nairobi_cbd: Tomato crate (Cebit) KES 4500, Onions (Red) KES 120/kg, Potatoes (Irish) KES 3200/bag.
market_trends: Saturday morning peak (6am-10am) in Gikomba. Digital payments (M-Pesa) usage up 20% in small stalls. High demand for affordable cleaning supplies.
business_advice: Limit credit to 10% of stock value. Always keep 3 months 'emergency hustler fund'. Focus on fast-moving goods during rainy seasons.
marketing_tips: Use 'Kitu Safi' signage for quality perception. WhatsApp Status is the best free reach for CBD retailers. Group bundles (e.g., 3 for 100) move stock 2x faster.
scaling_wisdom: Don't open a second location until the first runs itself for 30 days. Diversify suppliers to avoid 'out of stock' traps in Eastleigh.
gikomba_wholesale: Grade A Mitumba bales: Jeans KES 12k, T-shirts KES 8k. Best time for picking is Tuesday 4am.
